title: Mellow Filipino Peanut Kare-Kare
description: A briny, velvety, light Filipino dish featuring onion salt, agemono-deep-fry, and a flexitarian-friendly take on the traditional Kare-Kare recipe.

ingredients.name: broad beans
ingredients.notes: fresh or frozen, trimmed and cut into 1-inch pieces

instructions: Heat the vegetable oil in a large saucepan over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the ground peanuts and cook for 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add the ground rice and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the broad beans, onion salt, water, fish sauce, banana ketchup, and ground black pepper. Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the peanut mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean saucepan, discarding the solids.
Return the peanut mixture to a simmer over medium heat and cook until thickened, stirring constantly, about 5 minutes.
Dip a small amount of the peanut mixture onto the rice flour-dusted plate and fry until golden, about 2-3 seconds per side.
Drain the fried peanut mixture on paper towels and garnish with chopped coriander leaves and onion.
Dust with rice flour and serve hot.
